---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Lucia S. Layritz"
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero">
  <p class="tagline">Researcher · Writer · Data Scientist</p>
  <h1>Lucia S. Layritz</h1>
  <p class="bio">
    I study the impact of climate change on ecosystem regeneration after disturbances — 
    wildfires, armed conflicts, and other disruptions — by integrating ecosystem modelling, 
    remote sensing, and statistical analysis. My work aims to inform strategies for 
    climate adaptation and ecological resilience.
  </p>
  <p class="bio" style="margin-top:0.8rem;">
    Currently a postdoctoral researcher at the 
    <a href="https://dse.berkeley.edu/people/lucia-layritz" target="_blank">Eric &amp; Wendy Schmidt Center for Data Science and Environment</a>, 
    UC Berkeley. I like probabilities, maps, and making science look beautiful.
  </p>
  <div class="hero-links">
    <a href="science.html">Science</a>
    <a href="writing.html">Writing &amp; Editing</a>
    <a href="dataviz.html">Data Visualisation</a>
    <a href="photography.html">Photography</a>
    <a href="software.html">Software</a>
  </div>
</div>

```

### Current Position

Postdoctoral Researcher, [Eric & Wendy Schmidt Center for Data Science and Environment](https://dse.berkeley.edu/people/lucia-layritz), UC Berkeley.
Supporting the National Park Service Climate Change Decision Support project.

### Education

- **PhD** Life Science — Technical University of Munich
- **MSc** Mathematical Modelling — King's College London, UK
- **MSc** Environmental Science and Engineering — Technical University of Berlin, Germany
- Research stays: Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research · American University of Beirut
